rulings.Case Title: G. S. VS. CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD OF ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Plaitniff'S Opposition To California Physicians' Service Dba Blue Shield Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for Jan4uary 27, 2026, line 4. PLAINTIFF G. S.'S Motion To File Under Seal Confidential Information Filed In Support Of Plaitniff's Opposition To California Physicians' Service dba Blue Shield Of California's Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Continued on the court's motion to February 4, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. BADRI B SINGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 27, 2026, line 10. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance. Plaintiff Wells Fargo, N.A.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, to defendant Badri Singh is granted. Singh failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by Singh. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) Wells Fargo does not seek sanctions. But CCP 2033.280(c) provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $50 payable by Singh to Wells Fargo within 30 days of entry of this order. Wells Fargo shall provide notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BAYER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Consolidation Of Cases Cgc-22-600482 & Cgc-25-630667
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 27, 2026, line 11. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY BAYER'S Motion For Consolidation Of Cases CGC-22-600482 & CGC-25-630667. Plaintiff Jeffrey Bayer's motion to consolidate this matter with Bayer v. City & County of San Francisco, Case No. CGC-22-600482, is denied. Case number 600482 was removed to federal court, depriving this court of jurisdiction, and this court's docket does not reflect that it was remanded. Case number 600482 is therefore not pending in this court and the court may not revive its jurisdiction by consolidating the cases. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BAYER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for January 27, 2026, line 11. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. The City and County of San Francisco's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The City contends the action is time-barred because plaintiff's claims arise out of an interaction with police that occurred years ago. The court grants the City's request for judicial notice but cannot conclude from other pleadings that they discuss the same incident. Plaintiff's complaint does not state the date of his injury. The court may only sustain a demurrer on limitations grounds where the defect appears on the face of the pleading. The City contends that the demurrer is uncertain. The court disagrees: its factual allegations are specific. A complaint must be liberally construed with a view to substantial justice. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 452.) The court construes the complaint as pleading an intentional tort in violation of the Penal Code provisions specified in the complaint. If there are additional causes of action plaintiff wishes to plead, such as statutory causes of action, he has leave to plead them. The City contends that plaintiff has not complied with the Government Code claims-presentation requirement. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead either compliance or facts excusing his compliance. The City presents a declaration establishing that there is no public record of the filing of a timely government claim. This is a proper subject of judicial notice (see Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 376), but plaintiff may plead contrary facts if he can in good faith or he may plead facts excusing compliance with the claims-presentation requirement. If plaintiff wishes to amend, he shall do so by February 27, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
